from_states.description: Optional list of delivery states the row must currently be in for the transition to apply. When provided, the SQL update narrows to ``WHERE state IN (...)`` so a row that has legitimately advanced past the caller's expected source state in between the caller's SELECT and this POST is preserved. Callers SHOULD narrow to the specific state they expect (e.g. cron fail_stale_pushed → ["pushed"]). Omitting it accepts any non-terminal source state (the broad allow-list).

PatchOperation.description:
A single JSON Patch operation (RFC 6902 subset).

Represents one atomic change to be applied to a workout.

Examples:
    >>> # Replace title
    >>> op = PatchOperation(op="replace", path="/title", value="New Title")

    >>> # Add a tag
    >>> op = PatchOperation(op="add", path="/tags/-", value="strength")

    >>> # Remove exercise at index 2
    >>> op = PatchOperation(op="remove", path="/exercises/2")

    >>> # Replace exercise sets
    >>> op = PatchOperation(op="replace", path="/exercises/0/sets", value=5)

op.description: The operation type: replace, add, or remove
op.title: Op

path.description: JSON Pointer path to the target location (e.g., /title, /exercises/0/sets)
path.title: Path

value.description: The value to set (required for replace and add, ignored for remove)
value.title: Value

PatchOperation.title: PatchOperation

PatchWorkoutErrorResponse.description:
Error response for patch workout endpoint.

AMA-1667: `detail` is `object | array` because two distinct 422
paths share this status code:
- FastAPI/Pydantic body validation (e.g. empty `operations` array)
  returns `detail: list[ErrorItem]`.
- The PatchWorkoutUseCase rejecting a semantically invalid op
  returns `detail: {message, validation_errors}` (object).
PatchWorkoutErrorResponse.title: PatchWorkoutErrorResponse

delete.description:
Delete user account and all associated data.

This permanently deletes:
- All workouts
- All workout completions
- All programs and tags
- All follow-along workouts
- All paired devices
- Voice settings and corrections
- External service connections (Strava, Garmin)
- Calendar events
- User profile

Cascade: Supabase rows → Garmin token revocation via row deletion →
Stripe customer deletion → Clerk user deletion → LangSmith run deletion note.

External-provider cleanup is best-effort. The account deletion succeeds once
internal data deletion succeeds; provider failures are returned in
cascade_errors for observability.

get.description:
Get a preview of all user data that will be deleted when account is deleted.

Returns counts of:
- workouts: Number of saved workouts
- workout_completions: Number of completed workout records
- programs: Number of workout programs
- tags: Number of custom tags
- follow_along_workouts: Number of follow-along workouts
- paired_devices: Number of iOS devices paired
- voice_settings: Whether voice settings exist
- voice_corrections: Number of voice correction entries
- strava_connection: Whether Strava is connected
- garmin_connection: Whether Garmin is connected
- total_items: Total count of deletable items
- has_ios_devices: Boolean indicating if iOS app needs attention
- has_external_connections: Boolean indicating if external services connected

Get workouts pending sync to Android Companion App.

Called by the Android Companion App to discover workouts ready for sync
to Wear OS/Health Connect watches. Returns workouts where
android_companion_synced_at is set, ordered by most recently pushed.
By default excludes completed workouts.

Args:
    user_id: Authenticated user ID from JWT
    limit: Maximum number of workouts to return (1-100)
    exclude_completed: Whether to exclude completed workouts

Returns:
    List of pending Android Companion workouts with interval data

parameters.description: Exclude workouts that have been completed
parameters.name: exclude_completed

schema.description: Exclude workouts that have been completed
schema.title: Exclude Completed

get.description: Read back the current athlete profile for an authenticated user.

post.description:
Create or update the athlete's profile + kick off initial plan generation.

Gated by `require_pro_plan`: the caller must have an active Clerk
subscription item on plan slug `pro` (trialing counts as active during
the Clerk-managed 7-day trial). See AMA-1590 / backend/billing.py.

Confirm Garmin watch pairing (called by Telegram bot).

Looks up the short_code, sets clerk_user_id to link the watch to the user.
The watch's polling will then detect paired=True.

AMA-1551: now requires a valid auth credential via get_current_user —
Clerk JWT (Authorization: Bearer), X-API-Key, or the dev/staging
test-bypass pair X-Test-Auth + X-Test-User-Id. Was previously accepting
a raw X-Test-User-Id header only — usable by anyone who knew a user_id
and a valid 6-digit pairing code.

Generate a pairing code for the CIQ widget (NO AUTH REQUIRED).

The watch calls this on first launch. It displays the code on screen.
The user enters the code in the Telegram bot (/pair CODE).
The watch polls /pair/status/{token} until confirmation.

get.description:
Poll for pairing completion (NO AUTH REQUIRED).

The CIQ widget polls this every 3 seconds after displaying the code.
Returns paired=True + device_token once the user confirms via bot.

List available workouts for this device.

Returns workout summaries with queued status for the CIQ widget
to display in its list view.

schema.title: Response List Workouts Api Garmin Workouts Get

post.description:
Mark a workout as downloaded by the CIQ widget.

Called by the widget after successfully downloading the FIT file
and creating the PersistedContent.Workout for native handoff.

get.description:
Download workout as FIT binary for Garmin CIQ widget.

Returns application/octet-stream with the FIT file bytes.
The CIQ widget downloads this and creates a PersistedContent.Workout
for handoff to Garmin's native workout player.

post.description:
Queue a workout for download by the CIQ widget.

Called from the Telegram bot /push command or from the widget itself.
The widget polls this queue to know which workouts to download.

Confirm that the watch successfully loaded today's workout (AMA-1611).

Called by the Garmin CIQ widget after PersistedContent.Workout has been
handed off to the native player (the workout is "on the device"). Advances
the workout_delivery row from fetched_by_widget → confirmed_on_device.

Endpoint contract is locked per plan §5.h. The request body fields are all
optional today and reserved for future telemetry (battery, uptime, FIT
size); an empty {} body is accepted and recommended for the MVP widget.

Idempotency: confirming an already-confirmed delivery returns 200 with the
existing confirmed_at, so the widget can safely retry.

404.description: Workout not found or not accessible by this device

409.description: Delivery row is already in the failed state

410.description: No active workout_delivery row for (workout, device)

get.description:
Return all current-user data as machine-readable JSON.

Synchronous for MVP; per-table fetch failures are logged and represented as
empty arrays so one missing optional table does not block export. Per-table
rows are capped at _EXPORT_PER_TABLE_LIMIT (AMA-1668) to bound wall time;
`truncated_tables` lists any table that hit the cap so the client can fall
back to a paginated export.

schema.title: Response Export User Data Api Privacy Export Get

get.description: Poll whether the bot has consumed this specific token and linked the account.

503.description: Telegram linking store is temporarily unavailable

post.description: Generate a one-time token for Telegram ?start= deep link. Expires in 15 min.

503.description: Telegram linking store is temporarily unavailable

post.description: Capture a waitlist signup. Upserts on email — re-submits update goal_event_text.

Insert one workout_delivery row per device_token in `generated` state.

Optional ``Idempotency-Key`` protects daily-runner retries/timeouts from
creating duplicate generated rows. The DB partial unique index remains the
durable backstop if callers omit the header or Redis is unavailable.

get.description:
Return the latest delivery state for a (workout, optional device) pair.

Used by the mobile Home + Settings UIs to render the per-workout watch
sync status card. RLS on workout_delivery already restricts to the
caller's rows; this endpoint adds the explicit user_id filter as a
second-line guard so a service-role client wouldn't accidentally leak.

parameters.description: Optional. Restrict the lookup to this paired device. When omitted, returns the latest delivery for any of the caller's paired devices.
parameters.name: device_token

schema.description: Optional. Restrict the lookup to this paired device. When omitted, returns the latest delivery for any of the caller's paired devices.
schema.title: Device Token

404.description: No delivery row for this user/workout/device

get.description:
Return the latest delivery per device for "today's workout".

Convenience endpoint for Home — polls one row per paired device so the
UI can show a per-device watch-sync card. Returns 200 with empty
`deliveries: []` when no workout has been generated for the user today.

"Today" is currently defined as `generated_at >= now_utc - 24h`. The
plan acknowledges this is a UTC approximation; per-user TZ refinement is
deferred until the calendar-api scheduler (AMA-1616) lands its TZ helper.

post.description:
Mark a delivery as terminally failed with the supplied `reason`.

Called by daily-runner (push_retries_exhausted) and by the calendar-api
delivery scheduler (fetch_deadline / confirm_deadline) — see plan §2.5
and §2.7. Idempotent: marking an already-failed row returns the existing
row WITHOUT overwriting ``failed_at`` / ``failure_reason`` (CodeRabbit
feedback on PR #291, 2026-04-26).

Advance a generated delivery to `pushed` and set the 30-min fetch deadline.

Body is intentionally empty — the timestamp comes from the server clock so
the deadline scanner reads consistent values.

409.description: Delivery is no longer in 'generated' state

post.description:
Increment push_attempts and record the most recent error code.

Called by daily-runner during the 3× exponential-backoff loop (plan §2.2)
each time the push to the queue endpoint fails. Only valid while the row
is still in `generated`; the underlying service raises
TerminalDeliveryError once the row has moved past that.

409.description: Delivery is no longer in 'generated' state

post.description:
Create fresh delivery rows and re-trigger the push pipeline.

Per plan §2.8 / §5.h: each resend inserts NEW workout_delivery rows with
parent_delivery_id pointing at previous failed/confirmed rows. Fan-out is
atomic via ``fn_resend_delivery_batch``: either every target device gets a
new generated row or none do. Optional ``Idempotency-Key`` makes mobile
double-taps safe for 5 minutes.

404.description: Workout not found for this user

409.description: Current delivery state does not allow resend

422.description: User has no paired Garmin devices

post.description:
Stream chat responses from Claude AI with SSE.

This endpoint provides real-time streaming responses from Claude Sonnet 4.5,
including:
- message_start: Sent when message processing begins
- content_delta: Streaming text content as it arrives
- function_call: When Claude wants to call a tool
- function_result: Results from tool execution
- message_end: When the response is complete
- error: Error events (including rate_limit_exceeded)

Request Body:
    - session_id: Existing session ID to resume, or null for new session
    - message: User's message to the AI assistant
    - context: Optional context (current_page, selected_workout_id)

SSE Events:
    - message_start: {session_id, message_id}
    - content_delta: {content, message_id} (streaming text)
    - function_call: {function_name, parameters, message_id}
    - function_result: {function_name, result, message_id}
    - message_end: {message_id}
    - error: {error_type, message, retry_after?}

Args:
    request: Chat request with session_id, message, and context
    http_request: FastAPI request object
    user_id: Authenticated user ID (from Clerk JWT)

Returns:
    StreamingResponse with SSE format

get.description:
Return the user's clip queue, newest-first.

Pass ?status=pending|imported|dismissed to filter by status.
Omit the param to receive all clips.

post.description:
Receive a clipped URL from the browser extension.

The extension sends the page URL, title, any extracted workout text,
an optional thumbnail URL, and the hostname as *source*.

Returns the saved clip record.

delete.description:
Remove a clip from the queue.

Only the owning user may delete their own clips (enforced both in this
handler and by the Supabase RLS policy on *clip_queue*).

post.description:
Parse a clipped page's text into a structured workout.

Fetches the clip from the database, sends *content* to the ingestor-api
text-parse endpoint, and marks the clip as 'imported' on success.

The ingestor endpoint called:
    POST {INGESTOR_URL}/parse/text
    Body: { "text": "<clip content>", "user_id": "<user_id>" }

Returns the raw workout structure from the ingestor so the caller can
decide what to do with it (e.g. open it in the app for review).

get.description:
Test endpoint to verify GARMIN_EXPORT_DEBUG logging is working.

Only available in development environments.

Returns a simple message and triggers debug logs if enabled.
Get all exercises of the same type (e.g., all squats).
Get similar exercises to the given name.

post.description: Get exercise suggestions and alternatives from Garmin database.

get.description:
List canonical exercises with optional filters.

Filters can be combined. All specified filters are applied together (AND logic).

post.description:
Match a planned exercise name to a canonical exercise.

This matches to the canonical exercises database (used for progression tracking,
1RM calculation, muscle group analytics). Different from /exercises/match which
matches to Garmin exercises.

Uses a multi-stage matching approach:
1. Exact name match (confidence: 1.0)
2. Alias match (confidence: 0.93-0.95)
3. Fuzzy match with rapidfuzz (confidence: based on score)
4. LLM fallback (if enabled, confidence: based on LLM)

Returns the best match found, or no match if confidence is too low.

Match multiple planned exercise names to canonical exercises.

Returns matches in the same order as the input names.
More efficient than calling /canonical/match multiple times.

Match a list of exercise name strings to canonical exercise IDs.

Uses PostgreSQL match_exercises function which tries:
1. Exact name match (confidence: 1.0)
2. Alias match (confidence: 0.95)
3. Trigram fuzzy match (confidence: based on similarity)

Returns matched exercise IDs with confidence scores.

get.description:
Get top matching suggestions from canonical exercises.

Returns multiple candidates sorted by confidence.
Useful for showing alternatives to the user.

parameters.description: Exercise name to get suggestions for
parameters.name: planned_name

schema.description: Exercise name to get suggestions for
schema.title: Planned Name
Response Suggest Canonical Matches Exercises Canonical Suggest Get

post.description:
Classify an unknown exercise using LLM (Claude Haiku).

When search returns nothing, call this endpoint to get:
- muscle_groups, equipment, category, difficulty, alternatives

Results are cached in the exercises table for next time.

Match a single exercise name to Garmin exercise database.

Returns the best match with confidence score and suggestions.

Confidence thresholds:
- 90%+ = "matched" (high confidence)
- 50-90% = "needs_review" (medium confidence)
- <50% = "unmapped" (low confidence)

Match multiple exercise names to Garmin exercise database.

Deduplicates names for efficiency and returns results for each unique name.

get.description:
Fast exercise search using PostgreSQL full-text search + trigram fallback.

Target: < 50ms response time. No external API calls.

Uses tsvector/tsquery for full-text search on exercise name + aliases + muscles + equipment.
Falls back to pg_trgm trigram similarity if no FTS results found.

This replaces the old Wger API call which was extremely slow.

Get a canonical exercise by ID.

Args:
    exercise_id: The exercise slug (e.g., "barbell-bench-press")

Export a saved workout from the database to various formats.

This endpoint retrieves a workout by ID and exports it to the requested format.
Unlike /map/* endpoints which convert raw JSON, this exports persisted workouts.

Supported formats:
- yaml: Garmin-compatible YAML (auto-detects HIIT)
- hiit: Garmin HIIT YAML format
- zwo: Zwift ZWO XML format
- workoutkit: Apple WorkoutKit DTO format
- fit_metadata: FIT file metadata (for analysis)

Args:

User mappings take priority over fuzzy matching.

post.description:
Convert blocks JSON directly to Garmin FIT file for USB transfer to watch.

The sport type affects how Garmin displays the workout:
- strength: Best for pure strength/weight training
- cardio: Best for mixed workouts with running, rowing, ski erg
- running: Best for pure running workouts

Lap Button Mode:
- When enabled, all exercises use "press lap when done" instead of counting reps/distance

    Binary .fit file download ready for Garmin watch

get.description:
Get all APNs push tokens for the authenticated user's paired devices.

Used by chat-api to send push notifications after saving a workout.

post.description:
Register an APNs push token for a paired device (AMA-567).

Called by the iOS app after obtaining a device token from Apple.
The token is stored alongside the paired device record and used
to send silent push notifications for workout sync.

get.description:
List all paired iOS devices for the authenticated user (AMA-184).

Returns a list of devices that have successfully completed pairing,
including device info (model, OS version) and when they were paired.

delete.description:
Revoke a specific paired device (AMA-184).

This removes the device's pairing, requiring the user to re-pair
if they want to use the iOS app on that device again.

post.description:
Generate a new pairing token for iOS Companion App authentication.

Returns a secure token (for QR code) and human-readable short code (for manual entry).
Both expire after 5 minutes.

Requires valid authentication (Clerk JWT or API key).

Exchange a pairing token for a JWT (called by iOS app).

This endpoint is public - the iOS app calls it after scanning a QR code
or entering a short code. The token proves the user authorized the pairing.

Returns a JWT that the iOS app stores and uses for authenticated API calls.

Refresh JWT for a paired device (AMA-220).

This endpoint allows the iOS app to get a new JWT when the current one
expires, without requiring the user to re-pair. The device_id is the
iOS UIDevice.current.identifierForVendor that was sent during initial pairing.

This endpoint is public (no auth required) because the old JWT may be expired.
The device_id serves as proof of previous pairing.

Returns:
- jwt: New JWT token
- expires_at: When the new JWT expires
- refreshed_at: When this refresh occurred

delete.description:
Revoke all active pairing tokens for the authenticated user.

Call this if the user wants to cancel pairing or generate a fresh token.
Also useful as a security measure if tokens may have been compromised.

get.description:
Check if a pairing token has been used (web app polling endpoint).

The web app polls this endpoint after displaying the QR code to detect
when the iOS app has successfully completed pairing.

Returns:
- paired: true if token was used, false otherwise
- expired: true if token has expired
- paired_at: timestamp when pairing occurred (if paired)

Supports both JWT authentication and X-Test-Auth for E2E testing.

get.description:
Get exercises that the user has performed.

Returns a list of exercises where the user has at least one completed
session with weight data, sorted by most frequently performed.

Get the history of a specific exercise.

Returns sessions where the exercise was performed, ordered by date descending.
Each session includes all sets with weight, reps, and calculated estimated 1RM.

Get the last weight used for an exercise.

Returns the most recent completed set with a weight value.
Used for the "Use Last Weight" feature in companion apps.

Get personal records for the user.

Calculates records from all exercise history:
- 1rm: Best estimated 1RM (calculated from weight/reps)
- max_weight: Heaviest weight lifted
- max_reps: Most reps at any weight

Get training volume analytics by muscle group.

Returns total volume (weight * reps) for each muscle group
over the specified time period, aggregated by the specified granularity.

post.description:
Reset all user data without deleting the account.

**WARNING:** This endpoint permanently deletes user data.
Only available in test environments.

Security requirements:
- Must be authenticated (valid JWT or API key)
- Only works in test environments (not production, not development)
- X-Test-Secret header is optional (validated if provided for automated testing)

Deletes:
- All workouts
- All workout completions
- All programs and tags
- All follow-along workouts
- All paired devices
- Voice settings and corrections
- Calendar events

Keeps:
- Clerk user account (authentication continues to work)
- External service connections (Strava, Garmin)
- User profile entry

Complete workflow: validate exercises and generate YAML.

Defaults to auto-proceed with best matches.

AMA-1749 item 1: `auto_proceed` may be supplied in the JSON body OR as a
query parameter for backwards compat. The body value wins; the query
parameter is deprecated.

post.description: Process workout but require review of unmapped exercises (stricter validation).

Validate workout mapping and identify exercises needing review.

Per-user mappings are applied when the caller is authenticated; anonymous
callers get a no-op repo and skip the personal lookup.

Inject warmup, rest, and cooldown steps into a blocks_json workout.

Prefs fallback: request body → stored user prefs → system defaults.

Sync a regular workout to Garmin Connect via garmin-sync-api.

Uses the same exercise mapping pipeline as the YAML export
(map_exercise_to_garmin + add_category_to_exercise_name), so
Garmin receives valid exercise names instead of generic steps.

Respects GARMIN_UNOFFICIAL_SYNC_ENABLED environment flag.
Requires GARMIN_EMAIL and GARMIN_PASSWORD environment variables.

SECURITY NOTE (ISSUE 2 - AMA-589):
⚠️  Uses shared service account Garmin credentials (GARMIN_EMAIL/PASSWORD env vars).
All users share the same Garmin account for sync operations.
This is a temporary MVP solution. For production, implement per-user OAuth
or encrypted credential storage with per-user API keys.

Args:
    request: SyncToGarminRequest with:
        - blocks_json: Workout structure with blocks and exercises (validated)
        - workout_title: Name for the Garmin workout (1-256 chars)
        - schedule_date: Optional date to schedule workout (YYYY-MM-DD format)
    user_id: Authenticated user ID from JWT

Returns:
    Success/error response with Garmin workout ID if successful

Raises:
    HTTPException 400: Invalid request data (missing/malformed blocks_json)
    HTTPException 422: Invalid workout data structure (transformation failed)
    HTTPException 500: Garmin sync failed or credentials not configured
    HTTPException 503: Unofficial Garmin sync is disabled

get.description: Get workouts for the authenticated user, optionally filtered by device and export status.

post.description: Accept a Suggest workout. Idempotent on (user_id, client_generated_id).

Returns:

Returns a paginated list of completed workouts with basic health metrics.
Does not include full heart rate sample data (use single completion endpoint).

Args:

Returns:

Get incoming workouts that haven't been completed yet (AMA-236).

This endpoint returns workouts that have been pushed to iOS Companion App
but have not yet been recorded as completed in workout_completions.

Use this instead of /workouts to get a filtered list of workouts
that still need to be done.

Args:
    user_id: Authenticated user ID (from Clerk JWT)
    limit: Maximum number of workouts to return

Returns:
    List of pending workouts in iOS Companion format

post.description:
Schedule a workout. Idempotent on (user_id, client_generated_id).

Behaviour:
  1. If a row with the same (user_id, client_generated_id) exists,
     and `workout_event_id` is null in the request, return that row
     (created=false). True idempotent retry.
  2. If `workout_event_id` is set, UPDATE that row and bump
     server_version. Returns created=false.
  3. Otherwise INSERT a new row. Returns created=true.

get.description:
Return the user's non-deleted planned workouts in a date range.

The new iOS Home + Workouts surfaces (AMA-1792) drive their reads
through this endpoint instead of the legacy fragmented set
(`fetchWorkouts`/`fetchScheduledWorkouts`/`fetchPushedWorkouts`).

post.description:
Save a workout to database before syncing to device.

With deduplication: if a workout with the same profile_id, title, and device
already exists, it will be updated instead of creating a duplicate.

Delegates business logic to SaveWorkoutUseCase and queues for export.

get.description:
Search workouts using semantic similarity or keyword fallback.

Generates an embedding for the query via OpenAI and performs cosine similarity
search against workout embeddings. Falls back to keyword search (ILIKE) if
OpenAI is unavailable or embedding generation fails.

Part of AMA-432: Semantic Search Endpoint
Get a single workout by ID.

patch.description:
Apply JSON Patch operations to a workout.

This endpoint supports a subset of RFC 6902 JSON Patch for workout editing.
Operations are applied atomically - all succeed or all fail.

**Supported operations:**
- `replace`: Replace a value at a path
- `add`: Add a value at a path (e.g., append to array with `/-`)
- `remove`: Remove a value at a path

**Supported paths:**
- `/title` or `/name`: Workout title
- `/description`: Workout description
- `/tags`: Replace tags array
- `/tags/-`: Add tag (add op)
- `/exercises/-`: Add exercise to first block
- `/exercises/{index}`: Replace or remove exercise
- `/exercises/{index}/{field}`: Replace exercise field (sets, reps, etc.)
- `/blocks/{index}/exercises/-`: Add exercise to specific block
- `/blocks/{index}/exercises/{index}`: Modify exercise in block

**Example request:**
```json
{
  "operations": [
    {"op": "replace", "path": "/title", "value": "New Title"},
    {"op": "add", "path": "/tags/-", "value": "strength"},
    {"op": "replace", "path": "/exercises/0/sets", "value": 4}
  ]
}
```

Part of AMA-433: PATCH /workouts/{id} endpoint implementation.
